headline: Six months into Gaza ceasefire, phase two of Trump's 'peace plan' remains in limbo
description: The Israeli army has tightened its grip on the enclave and killed more than 750 Palestinians over the past six months. Humanitarian aid continues to fall short of needs, and negotiations over disarming Hamas have stalled.

headline: Elon Musk snubs Paris prosecutors' summons over X and Grok
US and Iran in diplomatic deadlock and naval escalation as ceasefire nears expiration
How Trump's lingering grudge threatens his appointment of the next Fed chair
description: A senator from North Carolina warned that he would not vote in favor of Kevin Warsh, Donald Trump's nominee to succeed Jerome Powell, as long as legal proceedings against the Federal Reserve's chair were still underway.

headline: Understanding the (suspended) bill against antisemitism in French Parliament
description: A bill presented by MP Caroline Yadan was supposed to be debated last week in the Assemblée Nationale. But it was pulled from the agenda after a controversy over its content.

headline: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
description: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.

headline: France's housing crisis is straining relations between landlords and tenants
description: Tensions have risen at every stage of the landlord-tenant relationship amid a nationwide housing shortage.

Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
description: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

headline: The gold medal for deception: How a professor created an international award out of thin air
description: In 2016, Florent Montaclair stepped into the spotlight when he received the Gold Medal of Philology, a sort of Nobel Prize for the discipline. In fact, the award was created by Montaclair himself. It took an investigation by Romanian journalists to uncover this hoax, which is now the subject of a judicial inquiry in France.

headline: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
description: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.
